_instructions: 36 questions grounded against the real corpus (data/sample_corpus).
chunk_index values were VERIFIED by locating each question's `_locate_hint` phrase in the
actual chunks (see eval/verify_gold.py). Re-verify if you change CHUNK_SIZE/OVERLAP and re-ingest.
gold_answer is provided for all questions to enable Exact-Match / F1 scoring.
`_locate_hint` documents the exact phrase used to pin each gold chunk; harness ignores `_` keys.
Run `python -m eval.verify_gold` to re-check all indices against the current store.

questions.question: What is DualCast and what problem does it solve?
questions.gold_answer: DualCast is a privacy-preserving, real-time AI commentary generation system for eSports.

questions.question: What sampling temperature does DualCast use for commentary generation, and why?
questions.gold_answer: Temperature 0.8, for controlled randomness that avoids hallucination while allowing variety.

questions.question: How many keyframes does DualCast's VLM sample per inference, and at what resolution?
questions.gold_answer: It samples 5 keyframes per chunk, resized to 640x360 and JPEG-encoded before being sent to the VLM.

questions.question: Which TTS provider does DualCast use and what does it cost?
questions.gold_answer: ElevenLabs API, at $0.30 USD per 1M characters (roughly $0.02 per hour at 60 WPM).

questions.question: How did DualCast solve audio-video synchronization issues?
questions.gold_answer: By muxing video and audio into a single WebM container in one FFmpeg pass, and setting client-side MSE to sequence mode so timestamps are ignored and chunks play in order.

questions.question: How does DualCast prevent one slow client from blocking broadcast to other clients?
questions.gold_answer: It releases the async lock before calling websocket.send(), so a slow or hung client's I/O doesn't block delivery to other connected clients.

questions.question: Why did DualCast switch from time.time() to time.monotonic() for frame pacing?
questions.gold_answer: time.time() is affected by system clock adjustments and caused frame sync drift over long streams; time.monotonic() advances linearly and fixed sync accuracy to about ±50ms.

questions.question: How does DualCast handle an RTSP stream disconnecting?
questions.gold_answer: It runs a reconnection loop that reopens the video capture and retries with short sleeps until the stream is available again, so the pipeline recovers automatically.

questions.question: What is DualCast's typical end-to-end latency from frame capture to client broadcast?
questions.gold_answer: Approximately 5-7 seconds from frame capture to broadcast reaching the client.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the NavDrishti smart stick?
questions.gold_answer: NavDrishti is an AI-powered smart stick application that assists visually impaired users with navigation.

questions.question: How does NavDrishti avoid repeating the same audio warning?
questions.gold_answer: A per-class cooldown so the same detection type won't repeat within 20 seconds, plus a global cooldown of at most one warning per second.

questions.question: What YOLO models does NavDrishti run for object and currency detection?
questions.gold_answer: yolov8m.onnx as the general fallback model, yolov8n.onnx for lightweight general detection, and a dedicated currency_model.onnx for Indian currency denomination detection.

questions.question: What factors does NavDrishti's risk scoring engine combine to prioritize warnings?
questions.gold_answer: Class priority, proximity, centrality, bounding box area, detection confidence, and stability across consecutive frames.

questions.question: What is the default risk score threshold NavDrishti uses to trigger an audio warning?
questions.gold_answer: 92.0 — the DEFAULT_RISK_THRESHOLD configuration parameter.
questions._locate_hint: Min score to trigger audio warning

questions.question: How much inference speedup does ONNX Runtime give NavDrishti over the alternative?
questions.gold_answer: A 40-60% faster inference speedup.

questions.question: What persistence requirement must a detection meet before NavDrishti issues a warning?
questions.gold_answer: It requires 3 or more consecutive frames, or 2+ seconds, of detection history before triggering a warning.

questions.question: What minimum hardware does NavDrishti require for edge deployment?
questions.gold_answer: A Raspberry Pi 4 or newer with at least 2GB RAM, ideally 4GB.

questions.question: In which development phase did NavDrishti introduce the separate TTS/audio warning subsystem?
questions.gold_answer: Phase 3: TTS & Audio Warnings, which introduced tts_layer.py as a separate process along with the risk scoring engine and cooldown/throttling.

questions.question: Which AI model does TerraVision use, and for what?
questions.gold_answer: Google Gemini AI, to provide intelligent urban sustainability planning insights.

questions.question: How does TerraVision generate a transformed image using Gemini?
questions.gold_answer: Convert the image to Base64, send it to Gemini with a transformation prompt requesting TEXT and IMAGE response modalities, then extract the generated image and text.

questions.question: What is TerraVision's fallback chain of image generation models, and why does it use multiple models?
questions.gold_answer: gemini-2.5-flash-image, then gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview, then gemini-3-pro-image-preview — used because different API keys have different quota access and it ensures a robust pipeline if the primary model is unavailable.

questions.question: How does TerraVision calculate a region's green score and heat score?
questions.gold_answer: greenScore is clamped between 12 and 98 from a weighted greenness value; heatScore is clamped between 24 and 96, reduced by green cover percentage and solar presence and adjusted by a pressure term.

questions.question: How many predefined city-wide green corridor types does TerraVision generate, and using what layout?
questions.gold_answer: 6 predefined corridor types, generated using a 5x5 grid layout across the city bounds.

questions.question: What does TerraVision do if the Gemini response can't be parsed as JSON?
questions.gold_answer: It falls back to a pre-computed, hardcoded diagonal corridor path with coordinate clamping applied.

questions.question: What is the required environment variable for TerraVision's Gemini API key?
questions.gold_answer: VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY, with GEMINI_API_KEY supported as an optional fallback.

questions.question: What post-processing steps does TerraVision apply to a Gemini-suggested corridor path?
questions.gold_answer: Path simplification to a max of 5 waypoints, boundary clamping to the region bounds, obstacle avoidance around trees/solar features, and deduplication of points closer than ~9 meters apart.

questions.question: What type of system is Vote Rakshak?
questions.gold_answer: A decentralized, blockchain-based voting system.

questions.question: Which API endpoint is used to cast a vote in Vote Rakshak?
questions.gold_answer: POST /vote

questions.question: How does Vote Rakshak's smart contract prevent the same face from registering twice?
questions.gold_answer: It requires that the faceHash is not already present in the registeredFace mapping before allowing registration, reverting with 'Face already registered' otherwise.

questions.question: How does Vote Rakshak's smart contract prevent double voting?
questions.gold_answer: It checks both hasVoted[enrollment] and usedFace[faceHash] before allowing a vote, reverting the transaction if either has already been used.

questions.question: Which endpoint handles the face-verification step of Vote Rakshak's admin login?
questions.gold_answer: POST /admin/login_face, which verifies the admin's face and issues a JWT token.

questions.question: How many stable frames does Vote Rakshak's FaceMesh require before auto-capturing a voter's face?
questions.gold_answer: 35 or more stable frames, followed by a 3-second countdown before auto-capture.

questions.question: What is the approximate gas cost of a vote transaction in Vote Rakshak?
questions.gold_answer: Approximately 150,000 to 350,000 gas.

questions.question: What is the name of Vote Rakshak's MySQL database, and what does it store?
questions.gold_answer: decentralised_voting — it stores voter info, admin credentials, and face encodings.

questions.question: What is the team name behind Vote Rakshak?
questions.gold_answer: Team Null Pointersss.
